title: Option<Option<T>> for nullable JMAP patch fields in PresenceStatusSetInput is correct
description: PresenceStatusSetInput uses Option<Option<&str>> for status_text, status_emoji, and expires_at. This correctly encodes three states: None = omit field from JMAP patch (leave server value unchanged), Some(None) = include field as JSON null (clear the value), Some(Some(v)) = include field with a value (set it). A future contributor might simplify this to Option<&str> to remove the double wrapping, but that would conflate 'do not touch' with 'clear'. JMAP /set patch semantics require the distinction (RFC 8620 §5.3). The doc comment on the struct already explains this; the explanation should be kept.
